examples: Occasionally his behaviour became so uncontrollable that he had to be placed in a straitjacket.
One of the things that keeps the party in its straitjacket is the tea-party movement.
The jury will have to be forced into an “intellectual straitjacket”, one law professor suggested.
One big constraint is that Europe is in a straitjacket of its own making.
They said the Fed had given itself another straitjacket - which it would live to regret.
